AgentDiagnosticsResponse.title: AgentDiagnosticsResponse
AgentDiagnosticsResponse.description: Diagnostics information for a specific agent.

AgentTaskStatus.title: AgentTaskStatus
AgentTaskStatus.description:
Status of a single agent task in a TaskListArtifact.

Named to disambiguate from sculptor.web.derived.TaskStatus (the
runner-level status with BUILDING/RUNNING/etc.).

AgentTypeName.title: AgentTypeName
AgentTypeName.description:
The per-agent type chosen at creation time.

`REGISTERED` requires a `registration_id` alongside it.

ArtifactType.title: ArtifactType
ArtifactType.description: Types of artifacts that agents can produce.

planFilePath.title: Planfilepath
planFilePath.description: Absolute path of the plan file the agent wrote in this turn, when the question is the synthesized ExitPlanMode approval. Set by `make_plan_approval_question` and consumed by the frontend ExitPlanMode tool block to render a click-to-reopen link. None for non-plan-approval questions or when the agent didn't write a plan file in the current turn (e.g. re-using a plan from a prior turn).

AuthResult.title: AuthResult
AuthResult.description: Result of a Claude authentication attempt.

AuthStartResult.title: AuthStartResult
AuthStartResult.description:
Result of starting an interactive Claude authentication session.

Authentication is two steps so it works in headless/remote environments
(e.g. a container) where the browser-loopback flow can't reach the user's
browser: start returns the sign-in ``auth_url`` and leaves the CLI running,
waiting on stdin; the user signs in and pastes the resulting code back via
:class:`SubmitAuthCodeRequest`.

On a machine with a usable local browser the CLI completes the loopback flow
on its own and no code is needed — that case returns ``success=True`` with
``needs_code=False``.

BabysitterAgentClaude.title: BabysitterAgentClaude
BabysitterAgentClaude.description: Always use a Claude chat agent, regardless of the workspace MRU.

BabysitterAgentMRU.title: BabysitterAgentMRU
BabysitterAgentMRU.description: Inherit the workspace's most-recently-used agent type (the default).

BabysitterAgentPi.title: BabysitterAgentPi
BabysitterAgentPi.description:
Always use a Pi chat agent. Only valid when the pi agent is enabled;
that validity is enforced by the resolver, not this model.

BabysitterAgentRegistered.title: BabysitterAgentRegistered
BabysitterAgentRegistered.description: Always drive a specific registered terminal agent, by registration id.

CodingAgentTaskView.title: CodingAgentTaskView
CodingAgentTaskView.description:
messages are the primary way of interacting with an agent.

this class is simply a way of deriving the current state of the agent based on the message log.

because agents are run as idempotent tasks, consumers MUST be able to handle duplicate messages.
this is particularly tricky because you cannot deduplicate on message_id here --
the ids may be different between two different runs
(and that cannot be fixed because different things may have happened)
consumers *may* process messages in a "task aware" manner, eg,
by paying attention to the task start and stop messages in order to properly discard outdated messages.

CommitDiffResponse.description: Response containing the unified diff for a single commit.

CommitFileInfo.description: Per-file change info within a single commit.

DependencyInfo.title: DependencyInfo

DependencyPaths.title: DependencyPaths
DependencyPaths.description:
Configuration for dependency binary resolution.

The ``claude`` field is a unified mode + path value:
  - ``"MANAGED"`` (default): Sculptor manages the Claude CLI binary.
  - An absolute path (e.g. ``"/usr/local/bin/claude"``): used directly.
  - A bare command name (e.g. ``"claude"``): resolved via the system PATH.

The default for ``claude`` can be overridden via the
``SCULPTOR_CLAUDE_BINARY_DEFAULT_OVERRIDE`` environment variable.  When the
user explicitly configures a value in Settings, it is persisted to the config
file and takes precedence over the environment variable.

The ``git`` field is an optional override path; when ``None``, git is
resolved from the system PATH.

The ``pi`` field is a unified mode + path value mirroring ``claude``:
  - ``"MANAGED"`` (default): Sculptor downloads and version-pins the pi CLI.
  - ``"CUSTOM"``, an absolute path, or a bare command name: a user-provided
    binary, resolved via the system PATH.

There is no migration validator for ``pi``: a previously persisted bare
``"pi"`` (the old default) is preserved and resolves via PATH (i.e. treated
as CUSTOM), so existing setups keep working after the default flips to
``"MANAGED"``.

fileErrors.title: Fileerrors
fileErrors.description: Per-file diff generation errors. Maps relative file path to error message.

GenericToolContent.description: Generic content for most tools - just a string

GitOperationResult.title: GitOperationResult
GitOperationResult.description: Result of a git operation (commit, etc.).

HarnessCapabilities.title: HarnessCapabilities
HarnessCapabilities.description:
Coarse-grained, bool-typed capabilities a harness advertises.

Read by backend feature gates and by the frontend (via the generated
TypeScript twin). Populated truthfully by each harness through
`Harness.capabilities()`. PHASE_5_NORTH_STAR §2 names this the
bool-field shape of the capability region; gated-method capabilities
(e.g. `Harness.is_ask_user_question_tool`) coexist on the `Harness`
interface for protocol-level questions no bool can express.

Fields have **no Python defaults** — every constructor must list every
field. When a new capability lands, pydantic validation forces an edit
at every constructor site (the base `Harness.capabilities()` body,
every concrete harness's override, every hand-built test fixture), so
the harness↔capability matrix is grep-complete: `grep <field>` finds
every harness's stance.

`supports_context_reset` and `supports_compaction` are distinct: context
reset is the `/clear` path that discards the session; compaction summarizes
the session in place at a threshold. They gate different surfaces.

`supports_chat_interface` is the coarse main-panel switch (chat interface
vs terminal panel), distinct from the per-affordance bools below it.

InitializeGitRepoRequest.title: InitializeGitRepoRequest
InitializeGitRepoRequest.description: Request to initialize a directory as a git repository

InstallProgress.title: InstallProgress
InstallProgress.description: Progress of an ongoing managed binary installation.

ListTerminalAgentRegistrationsResponse.title: ListTerminalAgentRegistrationsResponse

ModelOption.title: ModelOption
ModelOption.description:
One model a harness offers in its switcher.

`provider` and `model_id` identify the model on the harness's own terms
(pi sends them back as a `set_model` selection; for the Claude harness
`model_id` is the `LLMModel` value). `display_name` is the selector label.

isAutoCompacting.title: Isautocompacting
isAutoCompacting.description: True when the agent is auto-compacting context (detected via plugin hook).

NotificationImportance.title: NotificationImportance
NotificationImportance.description:
From the Apple Human Interface Guidelines: https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/managing-notifications

Passive. Information people can view at their leisure, like a restaurant recommendation.

Active (the default). Information people might appreciate knowing about when it arrives, like a score update on their favorite sports team.

Time Sensitive. Information that directly impacts the person and requires their immediate attention, like an account security issue or a package delivery.

Critical. Urgent information about health and safety that directly impacts the person and demands their immediate attention. Critical notifications are extremely rare and typically come from governmental and public agencies or apps that help people manage their health or home.

OpenInOsRequest.title: OpenInOsRequest
OpenInOsRequest.description: Request to open a file or its containing folder in the OS default application.

OpenPathInAppRequest.title: OpenPathInAppRequest
OpenPathInAppRequest.description: Request to open a file system path in an external application.

OpenPathInAppResult.title: OpenPathInAppResult
OpenPathInAppResult.description: Result of attempting to open a path in an external application.

PanelLayoutConfig.title: PanelLayoutConfig
PanelLayoutConfig.description: Panel layout preferences for the workspace page.

PiConfig.title: PiConfig
PiConfig.description:
Configuration for the pi agent harness.

Pi reads its API key from the user's process environment at launch.
``api_key_env_var_names`` lists the environment variable names whose
values are injected into the pi subprocess via the existing ``Secret``
machinery — the values themselves are never persisted in config.

PrApproval.title: PrApproval
PrApproval.description: A reviewer's approval status on a pull/merge request.

PrComment.title: PrComment
PrComment.description: An unresolved comment on a pull/merge request.

PrStatusInfo.title: PrStatusInfo
PrStatusInfo.description: PR status information for a workspace, streamed from backend to frontend.

PreviewBranchNameResponse.title: PreviewBranchNameResponse
PreviewBranchNameResponse.description: Resolved branch-name preview for the Add Workspace form.

Project.title: Project
Project.description:
A project is mostly a container for related tasks.  It has at most one git repository associated with it.

Note that the git_repository_url's are optional because it should be possible to run simple agents that do not require an `Environment` at all.

LOCAL_ONLY: For now, we should create a project with a file:/// URL whenever the server is started in some git repository.

ProjectEnvVarNames.title: ProjectEnvVarNames
ProjectEnvVarNames.description: Environment variable names loaded from a single project's .sculptor/.env.

ProjectInitializationRequest.title: ProjectInitializationRequest
ProjectInitializationRequest.description: Request to initialize a new project

ReadFileAtRefRequest.title: ReadFileAtRefRequest
ReadFileAtRefRequest.description: Request to read a file's content at a specific git ref.

ReadFileAtRefResponse.title: ReadFileAtRefResponse
ReadFileAtRefResponse.description: File content at a specific git ref, with encoding metadata.

RecentWorkspaceResponse.title: RecentWorkspaceResponse
RecentWorkspaceResponse.description: Workspace with denormalized project info and computed fields for cross-project listing.

SculptorSettings.title: SculptorSettings
SculptorSettings.description:
This class is for *server* settings *that do not change during runtime*.

If you want *user* settings or settings that can change while the application is running (eg most settings),
see `UserConfig` instead.

SignalEventRequest.title: SignalEventRequest
SignalEventRequest.description:
A terminal-agent signal.

`event` is a plain string so unknown events validate and reach the
handler (forward compatibility — a closed enum would 422 on additive
evolution). `session_id` accompanies the `session-id` event only.

SkillInfo.title: SkillInfo
SkillInfo.description: Information about a single Claude Code skill.

SkipAccountSetupRequest.title: SkipAccountSetupRequest
SkipAccountSetupRequest.description:
Request to complete the onboarding welcome step without an account.

The user keeps the anonymous, instance-id-based identity; the only choice
they make on the welcome step is whether telemetry stays on.

SubmitAuthCodeRequest.title: SubmitAuthCodeRequest
SubmitAuthCodeRequest.description: Submit the code the user pasted from the sign-in page to finish authentication.

TaskListArtifact.title: TaskListArtifact
TaskListArtifact.description: Task list artifact backed by Claude Code's per-task JSON files.

TaskState.title: TaskState
TaskState.description: The possible states of a server task.

TaskUpdate.title: TaskUpdate
TaskUpdate.description:
Represents an incremental update to task state sent to the frontend via SSE/WebSocket.

Initial Connection:
- Sends complete current state (all completed messages, current in-progress message, etc.)
- Provides frontend with full context to render the UI

Subsequent Updates:
- Only sends deltas (new messages, changed state, etc.)
- Frontend merges updates with existing state

Field Update Patterns:
- chat_messages: Only new completed messages are sent; frontend appends to existing list
- in_progress_chat_message: Sent in full each time it changes; frontend replaces previous value
- queued_chat_messages: Full list sent each time; frontend replaces entire queue
- updated_artifacts: Lists artifacts that changed; frontend fetches updated content

The frontend is responsible for:
- Maintaining cumulative state by merging updates
- Replacing vs appending based on field semantics
- Fetching artifact data when notified of updates

TelemetryInfo.title: TelemetryInfo
TelemetryInfo.description:
Information needed for setting up telemetry.

This data structure is generated once in the Sculptor server and served to
the frontend (which owns telemetry reporting).

TerminalAgentRegistration.title: TerminalAgentRegistration
TerminalAgentRegistration.description:
A registered terminal agent, validated from one TOML file.

The TOML body carries everything except ``registration_id``, which is
derived from the filename stem.

TerminalInputRequest.title: TerminalInputRequest
TerminalInputRequest.description:
An automated prompt for a registered terminal agent.

Smallest viable surface for v1: text plus whether to submit it — no
arbitrary key injection.

durationSeconds.title: Durationseconds

interactiveRole.title: Interactiverole
interactiveRole.description: Set server-side from the harness when the originating tool is an interactive-backchannel surface; lets the frontend suppress the result (it renders inside the question panel) by role rather than by tool name. None for a regular tool.

description.title: Description
description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool call does

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier for this tool use

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the tool being used

interactiveRole.title: Interactiverole
interactiveRole.description: Set server-side from the harness when this tool is an interactive-backchannel surface (ask-user-question / exit-plan-mode); the frontend renders by this role rather than by tool name. None for a regular tool.

TraceBatchRequest.title: TraceBatchRequest
TraceBatchRequest.description:
Batch of Chrome-JSON trace events from a non-backend source.

The ``source`` field selects the synthetic ``pid`` the events are tagged
with so that the backend's, the renderer's, and Electron-main's events
appear as separate processes in the Perfetto UI.

inputTokens.title: Inputtokens
inputTokens.description: Input tokens for this turn (None when interrupted before completion)

outputTokens.title: Outputtokens
outputTokens.description: Output tokens for this turn (None when interrupted before completion)

changedFiles.title: Changedfiles
changedFiles.description: File paths changed during this turn (git-relative, all tools including Bash)

contextTotalTokens.title: Contexttotaltokens
contextTotalTokens.description: Total tokens in the context window at turn end (None if unavailable)

autoCompactThreshold.title: Autocompactthreshold
autoCompactThreshold.description: Threshold at which auto-compaction fires (denominator for the percent display)

TurnMetrics.title: TurnMetrics
TurnMetrics.description: Per-turn metrics attached to completed assistant messages.

UpdateChannel.title: UpdateChannel
UpdateChannel.description: Update channel for receiving Sculptor updates.

UpdateUserConfigRequest.title: UpdateUserConfigRequest
UpdateUserConfigRequest.description:
Partial update for ``UserConfig``.

``user_config`` is a dict of only the fields the caller wants to change —
fields absent from the dict are left at their current server-side value.
The handler merges into the current config and re-validates as a full
``UserConfig``. This avoids the lost-update race where a stale
full-object PUT clobbers fields recently changed by another writer
(e.g. a debounced panel-layout sync overwriting
``enable_in_place_workspaces``).

UploadDiagnosticsRequest.title: UploadDiagnosticsRequest
UploadDiagnosticsRequest.description: Request to upload a diagnostics report.

UploadDiagnosticsResponse.title: UploadDiagnosticsResponse
UploadDiagnosticsResponse.description: Response after a diagnostics report is uploaded.

isPrivacyPolicyConsented.title: Isprivacypolicyconsented
isPrivacyPolicyConsented.description: Whether the user consented to our privacy policy

isTelemetryLevelSet.title: Istelemetrylevelset
isTelemetryLevelSet.description: Whether the user consented to our telemetry level

defaultLlm.title: Defaultllm
defaultLlm.description: Default LLM model for new agents. If None, then most recently used LLM will be used.

updateChannel.description: Update channel for receiving Sculptor updates (stable or alpha)

minFreeDiskGb.title: Minfreediskgb
minFreeDiskGb.description: The minimum free disk space before Sculptor will stop allowing new tasks and messages

panelLayout.description: Panel layout preferences for the workspace page

prCreationPrompt.title: Prcreationprompt

prCreationPrompt.default: Push my changes to origin and create a pull request. Check whether the repo uses GitHub (gh) or GitLab (glab) and use the appropriate tool. Write a clear description summarizing the changes.

prPollingEnabled.description: Whether to poll for PR/MR status at all. When disabled, the workspace banner shows the last cached status and stops issuing gh/glab calls.

prPollIntervalSeconds.title: Prpollintervalseconds
prPollIntervalSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to poll for PR status on open workspaces

prPollClosedMultiplier.title: Prpollclosedmultiplier
prPollClosedMultiplier.description: Closed workspaces poll every pr_poll_interval_seconds * pr_poll_closed_multiplier seconds. Crank this up to poll closed workspaces much less often.

prDefaultTargetBranch.title: Prdefaulttargetbranch
prDefaultTargetBranch.description: Default target branch for new workspaces

fileBrowserDefaultSplitRatio.title: Filebrowserdefaultsplitratio
fileBrowserDefaultSplitRatio.description: Default split ratio (percentage for diff panel) when the diff panel opens

fileBrowserTabCloseBehavior.title: Filebrowsertabclosebehavior
fileBrowserTabCloseBehavior.description: Which tab becomes active after closing: 'mru' (most recently used) or 'adjacent'

fileBrowserLineWrapping.title: Filebrowserlinewrapping
fileBrowserLineWrapping.description: Diff line wrapping: 'wrap' (soft wrap) or 'scroll' (horizontal scrollbar)

isAlwaysInterruptAndSend.title: Isalwaysinterruptandsend
isAlwaysInterruptAndSend.description: When enabled, sending a message while the agent is busy immediately interrupts and sends instead of queuing

commitPrompt.title: Commitprompt
commitPrompt.description: Default prompt sent to the agent when Commit Changes is clicked
commitPrompt.default: Stage every changed and untracked file, then commit with a comprehensive commit message. Do not leave any files unstaged.

ciBabysitter.description: Configuration for the CI Babysitter — watches MRs and prompts an agent to fix pipeline failures and merge conflicts.

pi.description: Configuration for the pi agent harness

envVarOverrideEnabled.title: Envvaroverrideenabled
envVarOverrideEnabled.description: When True, .sculptor/.env values override pre-existing environment variables

isSmoothStreamingEnabled.title: Issmoothstreamingenabled
isSmoothStreamingEnabled.description: Whether to enable smooth text streaming animation in the chat

isPanelLayoutPerWorkspace.title: Ispanellayoutperworkspace
isPanelLayoutPerWorkspace.description: When enabled, panel visibility and sizes are local to each workspace instead of shared

enableInPlaceWorkspaces.title: Enableinplaceworkspaces
enableInPlaceWorkspaces.description: When enabled, the in-place workspace mode is available during workspace creation

enableCloneWorkspaces.title: Enablecloneworkspaces
enableCloneWorkspaces.description: When enabled, the legacy clone workspace mode is available during workspace creation. Worktree mode is now the default; this re-exposes clone mode for users who still want it.

defaultWorkspaceBranchNamingPattern.title: Defaultworkspacebranchnamingpattern
defaultWorkspaceBranchNamingPattern.description: User-global default pattern for auto-generated workspace branch names. Supports <user> and <slug> placeholders. Overridden per-project by Project.naming_pattern.

workspaceBranchDeletionPolicy.title: Workspacebranchdeletionpolicy
workspaceBranchDeletionPolicy.description: What to do with a worktree workspace's auto-generated branch when the workspace is deleted: never (preserve), delete_if_safe (refuses to delete unmerged), always (force-delete).

enableReviewAll.title: Enablereviewall
enableReviewAll.description: When enabled, the Review All combined diff view is available in the File Browser

enableEntityMentions.title: Enableentitymentions
enableEntityMentions.description: When enabled, typing % in the chat input opens entity mention completions for repositories, workspaces, and agents

enableRichMarkdownRendering.title: Enablerichmarkdownrendering
enableRichMarkdownRendering.description: When enabled, .md and .markdown files in the read-only file preview can be shown as rendered markdown via the eye toggle. Off by default while we iterate on the renderer.

enablePiAgent.title: Enablepiagent
enablePiAgent.description: When enabled, the agent-type menus offer the experimental pi agent. Off by default. Gates only the creation entry point — an existing pi agent keeps running regardless.

enableFrontendPlugins.title: Enablefrontendplugins
enableFrontendPlugins.description: When enabled, the frontend plugin system loads runtime plugins and shows the Plugins settings section. Off by default. Enabling applies immediately; disabling takes effect after an app reload (already-loaded plugins are not unloaded mid-session).

defaultFastMode.title: Defaultfastmode
defaultFastMode.description: When enabled, new agents default to fast mode

defaultEffortLevel.title: Defaulteffortlevel
defaultEffortLevel.description: Default thinking effort level for new agents (low, medium, high, xhigh, max)

UserConfig.title: UserConfig
UserConfig.description:
Most configuration for user and for Sculptor app behavior should go here.

All required fields must be provided or validation will fail.

When you add a new field, you should add it as a field with a default value so that it is backwards compatible.

UserSettings.title: UserSettings
UserSettings.description: Settings for a locally stored user.

VersionRangeInfo.title: VersionRangeInfo
VersionRangeInfo.description: Version range configuration for Claude CLI compatibility.

warningType.title: Warningtype
warningType.description: Type of warning, i.e. name of the exception that was raised

Workspace.title: Workspace
Workspace.description:
A workspace represents an isolated working environment for one or more tasks.

In Phase 1, workspaces are implicitly created 1:1 with tasks. Later phases will
allow explicit workspace creation and multi-agent support.

The workspace owns the environment and provides a consistent execution context
that persists across task restarts.

WorkspaceBranchInfo.title: WorkspaceBranchInfo
WorkspaceBranchInfo.description: Current branch for a workspace's working directory.

WorkspaceFileEntry.title: WorkspaceFileEntry
WorkspaceFileEntry.description: A single file or directory in a workspace's file tree.

WorkspaceFileListResponse.title: WorkspaceFileListResponse
WorkspaceFileListResponse.description: Flat list of files and directories in a workspace.

WorkspaceGitOperationResponse.title: WorkspaceGitOperationResponse
WorkspaceGitOperationResponse.description: Response from a workspace git operation.

WorkspaceInitializationStrategy.title: WorkspaceInitializationStrategy
WorkspaceInitializationStrategy.description:
Strategy for workspace initialization.

IN_PLACE: Work directly in the user's repository.
CLONE: Work in an isolated clone of the repository.
WORKTREE: Work in a git worktree off the user's repository (shared `.git`).

WorkspaceRemoteBranchesInfo.title: WorkspaceRemoteBranchesInfo
WorkspaceRemoteBranchesInfo.description: Remote-tracking branches available in a workspace's working directory.

WorkspaceSetupOutputChunk.title: WorkspaceSetupOutputChunk
WorkspaceSetupOutputChunk.description:
Live output chunk for a workspace setup run.

`data` carries raw output bytes; Pydantic encodes them as base64 over the
wire and the frontend decodes back to bytes for display.

WorkspaceSetupSnapshot.title: WorkspaceSetupSnapshot
WorkspaceSetupSnapshot.description: Per-workspace setup snapshot embedded in WorkspaceResponse.

WorkspaceSetupStatus.title: WorkspaceSetupStatus
WorkspaceSetupStatus.description: Status snapshot for a workspace setup run.

post.description: Start a new task with the given prompt
Create a new workspace with project_id in the request body.

get.description:
Resolve the auto-filled branch-name preview for the Add Workspace form.

In-place mode returns an empty string since the field is hidden. Clone and
worktree modes resolve the project's or user-global naming pattern against
a `<slug>` derived from the workspace name (random if empty) and a `<user>`
slug derived from the repo's `git config user.name`.
List all workspaces across all projects, ordered by recent activity.

patch.description: Update a workspace's description and/or target branch.

get.description: Get a workspace by ID.

delete.description: Delete a workspace and all its agents (cascade delete).

post.description: Set is_open on multiple workspaces in a single transaction.

get.description: List workspaces for a project.

Get the latest diff for a workspace.

The diff shows changes from workspace.source_git_hash to current state.
Use force_refresh=true to regenerate the diff before returning.
Use scope=vs-target-branch to include the target branch diff.

Get the commit history for a workspace branch.

Returns commits from HEAD back to the fork point where the current branch
diverged from the target branch.
Get the unified diff for a single commit.

post.description:
Discard changes to a single file in a workspace.

Uses git checkout for tracked files and git clean for untracked files.

Open a file as the active tab in the user's diff panel for this workspace.

Emits an OpenFileUiAction event over the per-user WebSocket fan-out so
connected frontends update their per-workspace diff-panel atoms.

Drive the in-app Browser panel for this workspace to navigate to a URL.

Emits a WebviewCommandUiAction event over the per-user WebSocket fan-out so
connected frontends update their per-workspace browser-panel atoms.
Drive the in-app Browser panel for this workspace to reload the current URL.
Read a file from the workspace's working directory.

get.description: List all files and directories in a workspace's working directory.

post.description: Open a file or its containing folder in the OS default application.
Read a file's content at a specific git ref.

get.description:
Get available Claude Code skills.

Exactly one of project_id or workspace_id must be provided.

When workspace_id is given, discovers skills from the workspace's working
directory (which may be a clone in CLONE mode). Falls back to the project's
local repo if the workspace environment hasn't been initialized yet.

When project_id is given, discovers skills from the project's local
repository. Used on the Add Workspace page where no workspace exists yet.

post.description:
Create a new agent in a workspace.

If prompt is provided, creates and starts the agent (same as start_task).
If prompt is None/empty, creates the agent in a waiting state with auto-generated name.

get.description: List all agents in a workspace.
Resolve a TaskID prefix to a unique full agent id for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Delete an agent from a workspace.

post.description: Restore a failed agent.

get.description: Get diagnostics info for an agent (session ID and transcript file path).

post.description: Send a message to an agent via API interface.
Submit answers to an AskUserQuestion tool invocation.
Clear agent context.
Interrupt a running agent.

Switch a running agent's model (the pi out-of-band `set_model` path).

Used by harnesses with a backend model list (pi); Claude's model rides each
turn instead. The request blocks until the agent resolves the switch and
returns 400 with the agent's error message when the switch is rejected (e.g.
pi reports "Model not found"), so the frontend can toast it.
Run a single forked `/btw` Haiku turn that streams back via the unified WS.

get.description: Get an artifact for an agent.

delete.description: Delete a message from an agent.

get.description:
Returns telemetry info for the current user.

If the current user has not initialized their configuration, use an
anonymous config.
Check if user config exists and what fields are configured

post.description:
Save user email during onboarding

This function will determine the updated TelemetryInfo for the signed in user, and return that to the frontend.

Complete the onboarding welcome step without an account.

The user keeps the anonymous, instance-id-based identity (no email or
name); only their telemetry choice and the privacy-policy consent are
recorded. Telemetry events therefore stay anonymous.

get.description: Check if required dependencies are installed.

post.description: Trigger installation of a managed dependency binary.

Begin interactive dependency authentication and return the sign-in URL.

The CLI keeps running, waiting for the code the user pastes back via
POST /api/v1/dependencies/auth/code. On a machine with a usable local
browser the flow self-completes and the response has ``success=True``.
Submit the code the user pasted from the sign-in page to finish authentication.
Complete onboarding by saving config to disk and initializing services

get.description: Get the current user config

put.description:
Update user config — merges the provided fields into the current config.

``user_config`` is a partial dict; fields absent from it are left
unchanged. This prevents stale full-object PUTs (e.g. a debounced
panel-layout sync) from clobbering fields like
``enable_in_place_workspaces`` that a different code path just changed.

post.description:
Flip the binary telemetry consent.

This is the only endpoint allowed to change the underlying telemetry
flags; PUT /api/v1/config rejects requests that would change them.

get.description:
List directories at the given path for filesystem autocomplete.

Used during onboarding when no project exists yet. Returns directories only,
hides dotfiles unless the user explicitly types a dot prefix.

put.description:
Update the workspace setup command for a project.

Tri-state: ``None`` resets to the current default, ``""`` explicitly disables
the command, any other string is a user-customized command.
Update the per-project branch-naming pattern override. Empty string clears the override.

get.description:
List immediate contents of a directory in the project.

When ``workspace_id`` is provided the listing is rooted at the workspace's
working directory (e.g. a clone); otherwise it falls back to the project's
local repo path.  Absolute ``directory`` values bypass the root and list
the filesystem directly so users can reference files outside the repo.
Get just the current branch (fast endpoint)
Return whether `name` already exists as a local branch in the project's repo.
Get repository information including path and recent branches

delete.description:
Stop the pty + shell for the given workspace terminal index.

Distinct from WebSocket disconnect: closing the WebSocket preserves the
pty so the terminal can be reconnected. This route is for explicit user
"close this terminal" actions (the panel X button) -- it tears down the
shell, closes the primary fd, and removes the terminal from the registry.

get.description:
List the current terminal-agent registrations.

Re-reads the registrations directory on every call — that IS the
no-restart re-read the menus rely on; no caching.

post.description:
The local HTTP event API terminal-agent integrations post to.

Status events become ephemeral runner messages (run-scoped, no unread
tracking); `files-changed` refreshes the workspace diff; `session-id` is
validated and persisted for resume; unknown events are logged and ignored
so the vocabulary can evolve additively.

Write an automated prompt into a registered terminal agent's PTY.

The reverse channel that lets Sculptor features (Commit, Create PR,
custom actions) reach a TUI as if the user typed the prompt. Guarded so
text is only ever written to a program that expects it; works with the
terminal panel closed (server-side write, not the WebSocket layer).

get.description: Get all currently active projects for the session.
Read environment variable names from global and per-project .env files on disk.

post.description: Initialize a directory as a git repository with an initial commit.
Open a file system path in an external application.
Accept a multipart file upload and store it in the backend upload directory.

get.description: Serve a previously uploaded file by its file_id.

post.description: Bundle diagnostic data and logs into a zip, upload to S3, and return the report ID.

Buffer a batch of Chrome-JSON trace events from the renderer or the
Electron main process. Silently no-ops when tracing is not enabled — the
frontend / Electron main are responsible for checking that flag before
POSTing, but we double-check here to be safe.

get.description: Include StreamingUpdate in schema
Include UserConfigField enum in schema
Include UserUpdate in schema
Serve the static files from frontend-dist, serving "index.html" when no filename is provided
